name: Telegramtrigger Workflow

originalWorkflowJson.name: Telegramtrigger Workflow
originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Telegramtrigger Workflow. This workflow integrates 9 different services: telegramTrigger, stickyNote, telegram, switch, set. It contains 37 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Telegramtrigger Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Trigger".
Open the "Telegram1" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram2" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram3" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram4" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram5" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch_MessageType" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram6" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Menu" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Command?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Change status" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Start bot?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Register" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Payment Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Invoice" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch_Commands" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Global data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Trigger Data for Payment".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Trigger Data for Register".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Data for Invoice".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
